mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that drives wage growth and consumer spending, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding residential fixed investment indicate a synchronized recovery in durable goods demand and construction activity, which drives sustained earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5% over any rolling 12-month period, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects robust capacity utilization and pricing power, which compresses profit margins less than in early cycles and supports positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with forward equity returns in more than 60% of late-cycle observations, or the forward return spread between high and low INDPRO growth deciles falls below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations and tighter monetary policy outlooks, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances following a CPI spike above 0.5%, or the 5-day forward return distribution shows no statistically significant negative skew (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent and monthly nonfarm payrolls exceeding 150,000 confirm broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the cross-sectional momentum of the signal decays to zero within 30 days.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm employment reflects underlying economic momentum and consumer spending resilience, which positively correlates with corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.01, or the signal fails to generate a positive excess return after transaction costs in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing permits during a monetary easing cycle signal improved credit conditions and forward-looking builder confidence, which boosts near-term consumer sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST growth shows no predictive power for 5-day forward returns during rate cut periods, or the forward return spread between rising and falling permit months is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).
